content:
- refreshed Ralph task summaries, read choose-task rules, and checked done-task state
- confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks; current candidates are only non-bug tasks with <passes>false</passes>
- inspected the actual repo architecture: Rust workspace with crates/runner and crates/source-bootstrap, root scratch runner Dockerfile, verify-only vendored slice under cockroachdb_molt/molt, and a workflow that still publishes only runner plus source-bootstrap
- determined the next logical task is story-19 task 01 because the one-time setup image boundary is only partially implemented: source-bootstrap currently exposes only render-bootstrap-sql while runner still owns render-postgres-setup, so the foundational two-subcommand SQL-emitter contract is the right next step before downstream workflow and novice-path work
- next immediate action is to write .ralph/tasks/story-19-source-sql-emitter-image/01-task-build-a-one-time-sql-emitter-image-that-prints-required-sql-to-logs.md into .ralph/current_task.txt and quit

- created .ralph/current_task.txt because it did not exist
- wrote the selected next task path: .ralph/tasks/story-19-source-sql-emitter-image/01-task-build-a-one-time-sql-emitter-image-that-prints-required-sql-to-logs.md
- quitting immediately per choose-task instructions

- read prior progress, .ralph/current_task.txt, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill docs
- confirmed prior pass only selected the next task; no plan or implementation has started yet
- next: read the selected task markdown, detect whether it contains TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE, then either create the plan file and quit or begin execution

- read the selected task file and confirmed it has neither TO BE VERIFIED nor NOW EXECUTE markers
- determined this turn must produce a separate plan file, update the task to point at it, mark the plan TO BE VERIFIED, and then stop without implementation
- next: inspect existing task-plan conventions and relevant source-bootstrap/runner boundaries so the plan is concrete and executable

- inspected the current source-bootstrap CLI, image harness, README quick start, and the runner-side postgres setup/bootstrap code that still owns part of the setup surface
- identified the likely boundary smell for the plan: source-bootstrap owns Cockroach SQL emission while runner still owns PostgreSQL setup rendering, so the one-time setup contract is split across runtime and setup slices
- next: mirror repo plan conventions, write a plan that recenters all setup-SQL emission inside the dedicated image/crate, and update the task file with the new plan path and TO BE VERIFIED state

- inspected the story-19 sibling tasks plus both source-bootstrap and runner config models
- current design choice is the main architectural fork: rename the existing source-bootstrap slice into a broader one-time setup image, or keep the source-bootstrap name while moving PostgreSQL SQL emission into it
- next: pick the cleaner no-compatibility direction, write the plan file, update the task with the plan path, and stop immediately as required

- created the required plan file for story-19 task 01 at .ralph/tasks/story-19-source-sql-emitter-image/01-task-build-a-one-time-sql-emitter-image-that-prints-required-sql-to-logs_plans/2026-04-19-one-time-setup-image-plan.md
- updated the task markdown to point at that plan; left the task in pre-execution state and ended the plan with TO BE VERIFIED
- chose the bold boundary direction in the plan: dedicated setup-sql image owns both Cockroach and PostgreSQL SQL emission, runner drops manual postgres setup rendering, command configs stay separate per mode
- should do next after quitting immediately: review/approve the plan shape, and only then replace the plan ending with NOW EXECUTE before starting TDD implementation

- read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, the selected task markdown, and the existing plan file for story-19 task 01
- confirmed the task is still in TO BE VERIFIED state and the proposed boundary refactor matches the live code: crates/source-bootstrap is still source-only while crates/runner still exposes render-postgres-setup
- should do next, after quitting immediately due to context limit: finish reading the improve-code-boundaries smell references, sanity-check the plan against current docs/tests, and if the design still holds replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE then stop immediately

- verified the story-19 task 01 plan against the live repo state, including source-bootstrap CLI/tests, runner postgres-setup ownership, README quick-start, workflow publish targets, and all improve-code-boundaries smell references provided by the skill directory
- confirmed the plan’s core boundary decision is correct: the dedicated one-time setup image should own both Cockroach SQL emission and PostgreSQL grant emission, while runner should drop the manual grant-rendering surface entirely
- updated the plan file .ralph/tasks/story-19-source-sql-emitter-image/01-task-build-a-one-time-sql-emitter-image-that-prints-required-sql-to-logs_plans/2026-04-19-one-time-setup-image-plan.md by replacing TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE
- should do next, after quitting immediately due to context limit: start the execution turn by following the plan in strict TDD vertical slices, beginning with a failing public contract test for the dedicated setup image CLI and help surface

- read previous Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the improve-code-boundaries plus tdd skill instructions for this turn
- confirmed prior turn already moved story-19 task 01 plan into NOW EXECUTE, so this turn should read the task and plan and then implement in strict TDD slices unless the design no longer holds
- next: inspect the task markdown and referenced execution plan, then verify the live code still matches the plan before writing failing tests

- read the selected task markdown and the referenced plan file; the plan is in NOW EXECUTE state, so this turn must implement rather than re-plan unless the live code disproves the design
- confirmed the task contract: one dedicated setup image owns both Cockroach SQL emission and PostgreSQL grant emission, runner loses the manual grant command, output is text or simple json only
- next: inspect current workspace files and the relevant improve-code-boundaries smells, then start the first RED slice with a failing public CLI/image contract test

- inspected the current source-bootstrap crate, runner CLI, postgres_setup module, README, workflow, and image/test helpers; the repo is still fully on the pre-refactor split boundary
- chose the replacement public setup surface name `setup-sql` so the new one-time SQL emitter is explicit and not falsely source-only
- next: change the first public contract test to require `setup-sql` with exactly `emit-cockroach-sql` and `emit-postgres-grants`, run that targeted test to get a clean RED failure, then implement the rename

- completed the first tracer bullet: the package now builds a public `setup-sql` binary and its help surface exposes only `emit-cockroach-sql` and `emit-postgres-grants`
- kept the internal library crate name stable for now so runner-side harness code can continue compiling while the public boundary moves first
- next: add a failing behavior test for the shared output-format contract on `emit-cockroach-sql`, then implement the minimal typed format handling to pass it

- completed the second TDD slice for Cockroach output formatting: `emit-cockroach-sql` now has a typed `OutputFormat` boundary with default text and simple per-database JSON output
- reshaped the renderer away from one string bucket into grouped per-database SQL, which is the right boundary for the future Postgres command too
- next: run the full `setup-sql` package tests to expose the remaining old contract failures, then continue with the next failing public slice

- updated the setup image Dockerfile and image-contract harness to the new `setup-sql` binary and `emit-cockroach-sql` command; the full `cargo test -p setup-sql --test image_contract` lane is now green
- the next boundary move is runner-side PostgreSQL grant emission, which still owns the wrong public command and artifact shape
- next: inspect the runner contract tests around `render-postgres-setup`, write one failing public test that requires the setup image to own grant emission and runner to drop the command, then move the logic

- moved the manual PostgreSQL grant contract into setup-sql with a reduced destination-only config and removed the runner CLI subcommand plus stale runner artifact tests
- rewrote the README public contract around `setup-sql` for both Cockroach and PostgreSQL one-time SQL emission; runner readme tests are now green again
- next: finish the boundary cleanup by renaming the crate path away from `crates/source-bootstrap`, update workflow/test references, then run the package and runner CI-contract lanes again
